description: Repair cards distilled from Codex/system-relation analysis. Used to isolate system-graph knowledge.

cards.title: Preserve The PLL Feedback-Control-Lock Relation Graph
text: Repair PLL tasks as one coupled system: reference edges, DCO/oscillator edges, feedback divider edges, control movement, and lock assertion must all describe the same loop.
Preserve the public parameter and port interface exactly; derive private helper names from public parameters instead of replacing them.
Generate one oscillator/DCO cadence state, derive feedback from a divider/counter on that cadence, and compare feedback against reference edges.
Update the control monitor from phase/frequency error and tie oscillator cadence to that same control state; do not make `vctrl_mon` a cosmetic moving signal.
Assert lock only after the late-window reference/feedback ratio is stable; on ratio hop or frequency step, allow lock to drop and reacquire after the new relation settles.
Avoid independent shortcuts such as a free-running feedback timer plus forced lock. Those can satisfy one observable while breaking the system relation.

cards.title: Preserve The DWA Pointer-Window-Enable Relation Graph
text: Treat DWA as a state relation between sampled input code, pointer state, selected cell window, and scalar observable bits.
Keep one integer pointer as the only source of truth. Initialize it from reset/parameter state, then update it once per valid clock edge.
Decode the sampled input code into an active-cell count, clamp it to the cell array size, and form the complete cell-enable mask before driving any output.
For wraparound, split the contiguous window across the array boundary using modulo arithmetic. For no-overlap, advance the pointer so consecutive selected sets do not reuse cells.
Drive `ptr_*` as a one-hot view of the held pointer and `cell_en_*` as the held selected mask; expose scalar nodes with stable names for every bit.
Do not repair DWA by only changing save aliases or by independently toggling cell enables. The pointer, active count, wrap behavior, and no-overlap rule must stay coupled.

cards.title: Preserve The Edge-Order To UP/DN Pulse Relation
text: Repair PFD/BBPD tasks by preserving the mapping from two input edge streams to finite UP/DN pulses.
Record recent edge times or latch states for both sides. The leading side determines which pulse is emitted; the trailing side clears or bounds the response.
Keep UP and DN as independent held states, then clear them after a finite delay so pulses are visible but mostly non-overlapping.
For BBPD, data is an edge stream relative to the clock, not a static logic input. Retimed data and UP/DN pulses should come from the same sampled edge-order relation.
Keep all `@(cross())` event controls unconditional in the analog block and drive outputs with unconditional `transition()` contributions from held states.

cards.title: Preserve The ADC Code And DAC Reconstruction Relation
text: Treat converter repairs as a chain: sample/reset state, quantized code, internal trial/control code, and analog reconstruction must agree.
If code coverage fails, repair sampling/reset/code update first. If code coverage passes but output span fails, preserve the code path and repair code-to-output decoding.
Use one held integer code or trial code as the source of truth for all public code-bit outputs and DAC/reconstruction targets.
For SAR/CDAC/calibration cases, make the trial/control bits move during the sequence, then freeze the final state and assert ready/settled only after completion.
For differential DAC outputs, compute one differential signal from the decoded code and drive positive/negative outputs with opposite polarity around common mode.
Do not make `vout` track raw `vin` continuously when the prompt asks for quantized reconstruction; the analog output should follow the same code that drives the bits.

cards.title: Preserve The Serializer Load-Shift-Frame Relation
text: Treat serializer tasks as a state machine, not as independent toggles on `sout` and `frame`.
Latch the parallel input word only on the specified load event, then shift exactly one bit per valid clock edge.
Keep a bit index and a held shift register as the source of truth. The serialized output should come from the current index before the index advances.
Assert the frame marker only for the first serialized bit of each loaded word, then clear it for the remaining bits.
Preserve scalar observable names and drive `sout`/`frame` from held targets with unconditional `transition()` contributions.
